summary: Fixed the QA P1 boundary bypass by rejecting M7 and any non-M1-through-M6 entries in acceptedSaveManifests, constraining direct manifest classification to M1-M6 accepted saves versus M7 beta candidates, and adding a regression proving the reproduced M7 accepted-save boundary cannot return compatible.

tests_run.result: Final status shows allowed R2 changes only: M packages/save-format/src/index.ts; M tests/save-migration-cloud-boundaries.test.ts; ?? project/messages/outbox/M7-SAVE-MIGRATION-CLOUD-BOUNDARIES-001__MSG-20260629-M7-SAVE-MIGRATION-WRITER-R2-REVIEW__systems_architect.json. Pre-edit status was clean.
Task registry listed successfully; M7-SAVE-MIGRATION-CLOUD-BOUNDARIES-001 is IN_PROGRESS with systems_architect owner.
M7-UI-REGRESSION-MATRIX-001 DRAFT test_engineer Establish the M7 UI and localization regression matrix

tests_run.command: node -e JSON parse/shape check for M7-SAVE-MIGRATION-CLOUD-BOUNDARIES-001

tests_run.result: Vitest passed: 3 test files, 28 tests. Includes the new QA-reproduced M7 accepted-save bypass regression.
Architecture boundaries passed; deterministic Node/Worker save-load, M4, M5, and M6 hashes matched; root Vitest passed 42 files and 352 tests; web Vitest passed 1 file and 9 tests.
Electron security preferences passed; preload exposes only monsoonDesktop; renderer navigation restricted; allowed IPC channels remain desktop:getRuntimeInfo and desktop:setFullscreen.
Prettier format check, ESLint, recursive typecheck, and architecture boundary check passed.

decisions: acceptedSaveManifests is now constrained to M1-M6 only for the current M7 boundary; M7 entries in acceptedSaveManifests make the boundary invalid.
candidateContentManifests is constrained to M7 beta-content-lock-candidate entries with human-gate-required migration mode.
Direct manifest classification also ignores non-M1-through-M6 entries in acceptedSaveManifests, so an M7 manifest cannot be classified as accepted-save even if a caller bypasses boundary validation.
risks: This remains validation evidence only; formal M7 content lock acceptance, irreversible migration, cloud-save enablement, account-backed persistence, network persistence, telemetry, secrets, provider integration, and frozen save/platform changes still require Human Gate and security review.
requested_action: Re-review the P1 fix and regression coverage for the M7 accepted-save boundary bypass.
